hypothesis: If the Segment Anything Model 2 is fine-tuned end-to-end on a physics-informed synthetic LPTEM dataset spanning liquid thickness/SNR and resolution variations with box prompts, then it will outperform a U-Net baseline in IoU, F1, and J&F on diverse synthetic tests and yield centroid positions that agree with manual annotations on real LPTEM data because training on realistic physics-driven variability enables robust segmentation and tracking.
